 {"id":"bd-1rqs","title":"Unit tests: Extension Message & Session APIs","description":"Comprehensive unit tests for message injection and session control APIs.

## Test Categories

### pi.sendMessage() Tests
- Custom message creates correct entry type
- display=true renders in output
- display=false suppresses output
- triggerTurn=true initiates agent turn
- deliverAs modes (steer/followUp/nextTurn)

### pi.sendUserMessage() Tests
- User message triggers turn
- Content correctly formatted
- deliverAs modes work

### pi.appendEntry() Tests
- Custom entry appended to session
- Entry type preserved
- Data serialized correctly

### Session Metadata Tests
- setSessionName() persists
- getSessionName() returns current name
- setLabel() adds label to entry

### Tool Management Tests
- getActiveTools() returns enabled tools
- getAllTools() includes disabled
- setActiveTools() toggles correctly

### Model Control Tests
- setModel() changes provider/model
- getThinkingLevel() returns current
- setThinkingLevel() updates budget

### pi.events Tests
- emit() dispatches to handlers
- on() registers handler
- Handler receives correct data
- Multiple handlers for same event

## Test Approach
- Real runtime, no mocks
- VCR for any HTTP calls
- JSONL detailed logging

## Files
- tests/extensions_message_session.rs
- tests/fixtures/message_session/*.json","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:07.361517642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:02.423999015Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:02.423915670Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-1i5x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:48.313619638Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-2xgk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:49.504233476Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-2yoh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:51.900085689Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-3lv3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:54.335445454Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-m84r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:50.690412203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-vs72","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:53.109410321Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ry","title":"Impl: WebAssembly bridge for PiJS (QuickJS has no wasm)","description":"# Assumption / Constraint
Assume **QuickJS does not provide WebAssembly** (`globalThis.WebAssembly` absent). We still must run the full pinned sample set, including extensions that ship Emscripten-style JS+WASM bundles.

# Goal
Provide a **PiWasm bridge** so JS-tier extensions can use a **WebAssembly-compatible API** even though the JS engine lacks wasm.

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions**: the bridge must not branch on extension id/name.
- Behavior is determined only by: wasm bytes, import object shape, policy/capabilities, and deterministic runtime configuration.
- Sample set is only used to define the **minimum required semantics**, not to justify one-off hacks.

# Strategy (hybrid)
- Use **wasmtime** (same engine as Tier A WASM host) to execute wasm.
- Expose a minimal `globalThis.WebAssembly` polyfill (or equivalent injected module) that is **generically compatible** with common JS+WASM glue patterns.
- All imports that represent side effects must route through **connector hostcalls** and therefore capabilities/logging.

# Scope / Deliverables
## WebAssembly JS surface (subset)
- `WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports)`
- `WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming(resp, imports)` (if needed; otherwise extc rewrite to non-streaming)
- `WebAssembly.compile(bytes)` / `WebAssembly.Module`
- `WebAssembly.Instance` exports behavior used by typical glue code
- `WebAssembly.Memory` with `.buffer` semantics compatible with standard glue patterns
- Traps/errors mapped to actionable Pi errors + structured logs

## Import object handling
- Support JS functions as wasm imports with deterministic call ordering.
- Restrict / gate imports that imply OS access.

## Caching + perf
- Cache compiled wasm modules keyed by sha256(bytes + wasmtime_version + policy_version).
- Minimize cross-boundary copies; measure and optimize boundary overhead.

# Security constraints
- Enforce memory/table limits per extension policy.
- Ensure wasm cannot access host OS except via explicit connector imports.
- Structured logs: every instantiate/compile includes extension_id + artifact_id + elapsed + limits.

# Acceptance (hard)
- Any **wasm-using JS-tier artifact** in the pinned sample set runs end-to-end **without manual source edits** using this bridge (or Tier A when appropriate).
- The E2E harness passes **16/16** sample extensions.
- The implementation remains **generic** (no extension-id special-casing), enforced by tests/review.

# Tests (required)
- Unit tests: wasm compile/instantiate, memory growth boundaries, import call routing, trap mapping.
- Conformance fixtures for wasm-in-js scenarios with deterministic traces.
- Security tests: denied connector imports, exceeded limits, malicious wasm.

# Dependencies
- Must compose with unified capability model (bd-2hr) and dispatcher enforcement (bd-h04).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-1s8o","title":"Interactive: /reload shows diagnostics (models.json, resource warnings)","description":"# Goal
Surface important diagnostics after `/reload`.

# Required Diagnostics
- `models.json` parse error (if present) from `ModelRegistry::error()`.
- Resource loader diagnostics (invalid SKILL.md frontmatter, invalid prompt templates, etc.)

# UX
- Present as a concise warning panel or system message.
- Include file paths and short actionable guidance.

# Acceptance Criteria

-{"id":"bd-1shy","title":"Unit tests: http client streaming + timeouts (no mocks)","description":"# Goal
Add deterministic unit/integration tests for src/http/client.rs without mocks.

# Context
HTTP client powers provider streaming, but coverage matrix shows minimal direct tests. We need real-path coverage for request building, header parsing, streaming byte delivery, and timeout behavior.

# Scope
- RequestBuilder: headers, JSON body, timeout handling.
- Response parsing: status + headers, max header bytes, chunked and content-length bodies.
- Streaming: incremental body chunks + buffered limits.
- VCR path: RecordedResponse::into_byte_stream round-trip.

# Approach
- Use a minimal local asupersync TcpListener server with canned HTTP/1.1 responses (deterministic, no mocks).
- Capture JSONL logs + artifact index via TestLogger (bd-4u9).
- Document allowlist rationale in docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md if needed.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Success + malformed header + oversized header + timeout cases covered.
- No mock providers or fake tool results.
- Tests emit JSONL logs + artifact index.

 {"id":"bd-1tkj","title":"Conformance: plan-mode/ (multi-file command+event extension)","description":"Full conformance testing for the plan-mode extension — a multi-file directory extension that registers /plan command and tool_call event hooks to enforce read-only mode.  {"id":"bd-1tyz","title":"Task: Implement Session Hostcall Handler (pi.session)","description":"Objective: Implement runtime handling for pi.session(op,args) hostcalls so extensions can read session state and append data. Background: Session access per EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE.md §12.6 (getMessages/getState/getFile) and FEATURE_PARITY.md (get_state/get_messages/set_name); ext-compat notes include append entry + set name. Supported ops (case-insensitive): get_state, get_messages, get_entries, get_branch, get_file, get_name, set_name, append_message, append_entry. Implementation: dispatch in src/extensions.rs and src/extension_dispatcher.rs; normalize ops and map invalid payloads to invalid_request; derive get_file/get_name from state. Tests: unit tests in src/extension_dispatcher.rs cover get_state/get_file/get_name/set_name, get_messages/entries/branch, append_message, append_entry, unknown op error, invalid append_message error. E2E: JSONL capture/replay tracked in bd-6vcm.","acceptance_criteria":"[x] Session ops dispatched in src/extensions.rs; [x] Session ops dispatched in src/extension_dispatcher.rs; [x] Unit tests listed above pass; [x] Conformance coverage tracked in bd-6vcm; [x] cargo check --all-targets passes; [x] cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes; [x] cargo fmt --check passes","notes":"Implemented session hostcall dispatch and unit coverage; ran cargo fmt/check/clippy and targeted tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:53:54.241337486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:42:52.154764699Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:42:52.154583942Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tyz","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:53:54.252923317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tyz","depends_on_id":"bd-389q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:54:20.182035743Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1tz","title":"Unit tests: connector dispatcher + policy engine","description":"Background:
- The connector dispatcher is the security boundary; policy enforcement must be correct.

Steps:
- Unit tests for hostcall routing (read/write/exec/http/env).
- Policy mode tests (strict/prompt/permissive) with allow/deny outcomes.
- Audit log tests (ensure allow/deny entries contain correlation IDs and reason).

Logging requirements:
- Tests must assert log content and redaction of secrets.

Acceptance:
- All policy branches covered; logs are deterministic and audited.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

Gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test ✅","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:05:27Z"},{"id":240,"issue_id":"bd-1tz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed core unit tests for JS hostcall dispatcher policy + audit logging.

- src/extensions.rs: added CaptureLayer + tests covering host_call.start / policy.decision / host_call.end events; verifies call_id/extension_id/reason + params_hash; asserts secrets never appear in logged fields.
- Covers Prompt policy prompting + cache (prompt_user_allow -> prompt_cache_allow), Strict deny path (not_in_default_caps), and allowed tool routing via write/read.
- Existing integration tests in tests/extensions_manifest.rs already cover ExtensionPolicy::evaluate modes + required_capability_for_host_call mapping.

Commits: 12972f7 (http URL log redaction helper+tests), 3e76a31 (hostcall audit-log tests + non-panicking asserts).

Gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test ✅
E2E/JSONL artifacts: N/A (unit-test-only bead).","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:53:43Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ev","title":"Benchmark suite: startup/memory/streaming budgets","description":"Background:
- Performance targets: startup <100ms, binary <20MB, idle memory <50MB, smooth streaming (README + AGENTS).
- BENCHMARKS.md covers truncation/SSE baseline but not end-to-end budgets.

Scope / Steps:
1) Define benchmark harness for startup time (cold/warm), idle RSS, SSE throughput, tool-call latency.
2) Capture baseline numbers and encode budgets in BENCHMARKS.md + CI checks.
3) Add a legacy baseline comparison where feasible (pi-mono CLI timing).
4) Document measurement methodology + variance handling.

Acceptance:
- Benchmarks run locally and in CI with stable outputs.
- Budget regressions fail CI with clear diagnostics.
- BENCHMARKS.md includes methodology + latest baseline.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-3f0o","title":"Unit: tools error paths + filesystem failures (no mocks)","description":"# Goal
Close error-path and edge-case coverage for all built-in tools (read/write/edit/grep/find/ls/bash) without mocks.

# Why / User Impact
Tool failures are the most common real-world UX pain. These tests ensure users get consistent errors, helpful metadata, and safe truncation behavior across OSes.

# Scope (Granular)
- **Read**: missing path, permission denied, binary content, max-bytes/lines truncation metadata, head/tail markers, and `details` fields.
- **Write**: permission denied, nested dir creation, overwrite semantics, byte-count accuracy for unicode/UTF‑8, and truncation metadata.
- **Edit**: oldText missing/ambiguous, file not found, large file truncation behavior, and precise diff preview fields.
- **Grep/Find/LS**: limit/ordering invariants, max line length, gitignore behavior, and truncation markers.
- **Bash**: command-not-found, timeout, non-zero exit, signal kill, process-tree cleanup metadata, full_output_path behavior.

# Logging / Artifacts
- Use `TestLogger`; record stdout/stderr, temp files, and command lines as artifacts.
- Log input params + expected/actual `details` fields for each tool call.
- Capture any stderr warnings and include them in failure context.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Tests live in `tests/tools_*` (or existing suites) and use **no mocks/fake providers**.
- Deterministic on CI (no network, avoid race-y sleeps beyond minimal).
- Each tool’s primary error variants covered with assertions on `ToolOutput.details` + content markers.
- Coverage matrix updated in test comments (link to bd-1nn audit if available).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:57:45.393237949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:01.376404444Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T06:39:49.780499556Z","close_reason":"Completed: tool error-path coverage + gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3f0o","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:57:45.418853031Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":218,"issue_id":"bd-3f0o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan (scoped from current code/tests):
- Existing `tests/tools_conformance.rs` only covers happy paths + a few basic errors. Missing error-path + details assertions across tools.

Proposed additions (no mocks, temp dirs only):
1) ReadTool:
   - Permission denied on file (chmod 000) -> Error::tool("read", ...).
   - Offset beyond EOF -> exact error string + total lines.
   - First-line exceeds MAX_BYTES path -> special guidance message + `details.truncation.first_line_exceeds_limit=true`.
   - Truncation path -> ensure details.truncation present + continuation hint.
   - Image read with `block_images=true` -> explicit error.
2) WriteTool:
   - Permission denied (read-only dir) -> error contains "Failed to persist" or "create" path.
   - UTF-16 byte count parity (emoji) -> output bytes == encode_utf16 count.
   - Nested dir creation failure path (parent unwritable).
3) EditTool:
   - Missing file / permission denied -> "File not found" legacy msg.
   - Multiple occurrences -> error message contains "multiple" (confirm exact).
   - Fuzzy match path (normalize quotes/dashes) -> success + diff present.
4) BashTool:
   - Command not found / non-zero exit -> error contains exit code; ensure `details` absent unless truncation.
   - Timeout -> error includes timeout + `cancelled` path; verify no fullOutputPath if not truncated.
   - Truncation -> `details.fullOutputPath` + `details.truncation.*`.
5) GrepTool:
   - rg missing path (simulate PATH without rg) -> error message.
   - Invalid search path -> error contains "Cannot access path".
   - Truncate long line -> "... [truncated]" marker + details when limit/bytes triggered.
6) FindTool:
   - fd missing -> error message.
   - Path not found -> error.
   - .gitignore respected + limit/truncation details.
7) LsTool:
   - Not a directory + permission denied path.
   - Limit/truncation details.

Implementation notes:
- Use `tests/common/harness.rs` TestLogger for artifact capture.
- Add tests under `tests/tools_error_paths.rs` or extend `tests/tools_conformance.rs` with new modules.
- Keep OS-specific permission tests gated (skip on Windows/CI if perms unsupported).

-{"id":"bd-3g6a","title":"Download remaining extensions from web search results","description":"# Download remaining extensions from web search results

## Context
Multiple search agents found additional extension repos and npm packages not yet in our artifacts:
- GitHub code search found 70+ repos with ExtensionAPI patterns
- npm search found @oh-my-pi scope (10 packages by can1357), and other packages
- Awesome list mining found additional community repos

Some of these have already been downloaded, but gaps remain. This task fills those gaps.

## What To Do
1. Compile results from all search agents into a deduplicated list
2. For each new repo/package not already in artifacts/:
   a. Clone/download the extension source
   b. Copy relevant .ts files to appropriate artifacts/ subdirectory
   c. Generate SHA256SUMS for new files
3. Update docs/extension-master-catalog.json with new entries

## Key Sources Already Searched
- GitHub: registerTool, ExtensionAPI, pi.events patterns
- npm: pi-extension, pi-agent-extension, @oh-my-pi, author-based search
- Awesome lists: awesome-pi, awesome-ai-coding, etc.

## Important: Validation Before Inclusion
Just because a file mentions ExtensionAPI does not make it a conformance target. Files must:
- Be actual extension entry points (not tests, docs, or utilities)
- Use the pi ExtensionAPI pattern (not a different tool with similar API names)
- Be loadable (at minimum pass static pattern check)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All known gaps filled (repos identified but not yet downloaded)
- New entries added to master catalog
- SHA256SUMS updated for all artifact directories","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:18:19.494273927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:28:49.450545803Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3g6a","depends_on_id":"bd-2dnl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:18:19.510142414Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":314,"issue_id":"bd-3g6a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SEARCH AGENT RESULTS (2026-02-05):

Agent ae307c8 identified 24 repos NOT yet in artifacts:
HIGH PRIORITY (multi-extension repos, 30+ TS files):
1. mitsuhiko/agent-stuff - 10+ extensions (todos, files, review, control, answer, loop, cwd-history)
2. hjanuschka/shitty-extensions - 14+ extensions (plan-mode, ultrathink, usage-bar, clipboard)
3. tmustier/pi-extensions - 8+ extensions (files-widget, tab-status, arcade games)
4. nicobailon/pi-subagents - 16+ TS files (registerTool, events_api)
5. nicobailon/pi-mcp-adapter - 11 TS files (registerFlag, registerTool, ui_overlay)
6. nicobailon/pi-interactive-shell - 10+ TS files (registerTool, events_api, ui_overlay)

MEDIUM PRIORITY:
7-12. nicobailon-pi-interview-tool, nicobailon-pi-powerline-footer, nicobailon-pi-rewind-hook, prateekmedia-pi-hooks, qualisero-rhubarb-pi, mikeastock-agents, mikeyobrien-rho

LOWER PRIORITY (need verification):
13-24. Dwsy-ace-tool-skill, Dwsy-knowledge-builder-extension, gturkoglu-pi-codex-apply-patch, gturkoglu-pi-dynsys, sids-pi-extensions, tmustier-pi-nes, mrexodia-pi-cost-dashboard, ferologics-pi-notify, svkozak-pi-acp, aadishv-dotfiles, nicobailon-mcp-to-pi-tools

EXCLUDED from conformance:
- @oh-my-pi packages (can1357): Framework forks, NOT extensions
- Theme repos (zenobi-us/pi-rose-pine, catppuccin): No extension code
- Skills-only repos (badlogic/pi-skills, mxyhi/ok-skills): Not TypeScript ExtensionAPI
- ok-skills (mxyhi): Skills collection, not extensions

Agent a085676 compiled comprehensive ecosystem report: 100+ distinct extensions across all sources. Key API patterns confirmed: registerTool (very high), pi.on (very high), registerCommand (high), registerShortcut (medium), registerFlag (low-medium), registerProvider (medium), ui.* (high).","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:28:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3gly","title":"Research: curated lists (awesome/blogs/forums)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:33.112964402Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:00.400136089Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["curation","extensions","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gly","depends_on_id":"bd-19rf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:49.385004890Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gly","depends_on_id":"bd-3jxt","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:07:36.735811990Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gly","depends_on_id":"bd-d7gn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:33.152107256Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"}],"comments":[{"id":300,"issue_id":"bd-3gly","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"Goal
- Catch “high-signal” extensions that show up in human-curated lists, blog posts, and forum threads (often the ones people actually recommend/use).

Sources to sweep (examples)
- GitHub awesome lists that mention Pi Agent / buildwithpi / pi-mono.
- OpenClaw/ClawHub documentation pages that list featured extensions.
- Community posts (blog posts, release notes, forum threads) that link to extension repos/gists.

Process
1) Collect links (repo/gist/npm/marketplace) and record where each came from.
2) Validate each link as a true Pi extension via code signature.
3) Add validated candidates to the inventory with “discovered_via=curated_list/<source>”.

Acceptance criteria
- >= 10 curated sources swept.
- >= 30 new validated candidates added OR a written explanation of overlap with already-known sources.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:49Z"},{"id":368,"issue_id":"bd-3gly","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"TESTING + LOGGING REQUIREMENTS

Curated lists are messy (markdown, HTML, stale links). Make ingestion robust.

Unit tests
- Parser tests for:
  - markdown link extraction
  - de-dup across repeated references
  - stale/broken link handling (classified as “unreachable” not silently dropped)

E2E script
- Offline E2E that consumes a fixture set of curated pages and emits a normalized candidate list.

Logging
- JSONL logs must include:
  - source page URL + retrieval timestamp
  - link extraction counts
  - per-link validation status (reachable, redirected, dead)
  - classification decision (true Pi extension vs out-of-scope) and reason
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3h8w","title":"Risk review: license + security + runtime dependencies","description":"# Goal
Filter or flag candidates with licensing, security, or runtime‑dependency risks.

# Deliverables
- License compatibility check (MIT/BSD/Apache preferred; note GPL/copyleft).
- Security red flags (abandoned repos, unsafe install scripts, opaque binaries).
- Runtime dependency risks (Node/Bun requirements vs Pi constraints).

# Notes
This review informs final inclusion/exclusion decisions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:48.290384961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:20:56.330144740Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h8w","depends_on_id":"bd-250p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:20:56.330094676Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3h8w","depends_on_id":"bd-38dx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:42.425523760Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3h8w","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:48.303416529Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":335,"issue_id":"bd-3h8w","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Some extensions are popular but risky (license conflicts, unsafe install scripts, heavy runtimes).

Reasoning: A risk filter avoids validating extensions we cannot safely ship or support.

Considerations: Document any risky items kept for coverage reasons and include mitigation notes.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3hp","title":"TUI interactive e2e via tmux capture with deterministic artifacts","description":"# Goal
Deterministic TUI E2E via tmux capture with normalized logs and artifacts.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Launch interactive mode in fixed 80x24 tmux session.
- Drive a scripted sequence (prompt, tool call, slash command).
- Capture pane output + optional screenshots per step.
- Normalize logs/paths and emit JSONL artifacts for diffing.

# Acceptance
- Deterministic output in CI (VCR playback).
- Artifacts include keystrokes, pane dumps, and timing metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test

Log keystrokes + outputs to artifact dir for diffs.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:20:40Z"},{"id":245,"issue_id":"bd-3hp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented TUI interactive E2E tests via tmux capture. Created tests/common/tmux.rs (shared TmuxInstance + TuiSession with step tracking, pane capture, JSONL artifacts) and tests/e2e_tui.rs (7 tests: startup_and_exit, help_command, model_command, clear_command, multi_command_sequence, ctrl_d_exit, artifact_format). All 20 tests pass. JSONL artifacts include tui-steps.jsonl, tui-log.jsonl, tui-artifacts.jsonl, and per-step pane snapshots.","created_at":"2026-02-05T04:52:03Z"}]}
